summary: Joel Spolsky shares logistics updates for the upcoming FogBugz World Tour, a multi-city product demo event spanning the US, Canada, New Zealand, Australia, and Europe. He describes the planning challenges including booking hotel meeting rooms across roughly 20 cities and notes the surprising lack of internet-based booking for hotel event spaces. The post is primarily a gear-shopping request, asking readers for recommendations on projectors, PA systems, and subnotebooks for six weeks of road travel.
key_points: The FogBugz World Tour will cover approximately 20 cities across multiple continents, requiring about six weeks of travel
Hotel event coordination is still heavily fax-based, which Joel identifies as a business opportunity ripe for internet disruption
Joel is crowdsourcing gear recommendations from his readers, treating his blog audience as a trusted advisory network
The practical constraints of a road show shape technology choices: weight, brightness, portability, and durability all matter
money_quotes: There's a huge business opportunity there–getting hotel event coordinators onto the Internet.
I'm looking at about six weeks on the road.
Today she faxed around about 80 RFPs to hotels to get meeting rooms lined up.
Is there such a thing as a video projector which connects to the PC using Cat-5 instead of video cable, so I don't have to carry a 50 foot video cable around?
Does anyone have any experience (good or bad) with the new flash-drive based notebooks?
key_insight: Even in 2007, significant parts of the business world like hotel event booking remained stubbornly offline, representing overlooked opportunities for internet disruption.
